EDCS-04901135.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Parthico pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis VII consuli proconsuli patri patriae Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti filio res publica Thamusidensium pro salute Augustorum ex decreto ordinis posuit sub TE o procuratore\n
EDCS-04901135.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Parthicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 7th time, consul, proconsul, father of the fatherland, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus; the state of the Thamusidenses erected this for the welfare of the Augusti, by decree of the council, under TE o, the procurator.
EDCS-04901135.summary: This is a dedication by the city of Thamusidenses to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus for his welfare, erected by decree of the council and overseen by a procurator, likely from the Severan period.

EDCS-05000871.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute et incolumitate dddominorum nnnostrorum Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti et Publi Septimi Getae Augusti et Iuliae matris Augustorum et castrorum totiusque domus divinae procurante [] procuratore eorum Valerius Matius decurio alae III Asturum praepositus castelli Tamudensis cultum produxit III Idus Apriles Faustino et Rufino consulibus et deinceps observabimus\n
EDCS-05000871.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety and well-being of our lords Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, and Publius Septimius Geta Augustus, and Julia, mother of the emperors and the camp, and the entire divine family. Valerius Matius, decurion of the III Cohort of Asturians, prefect of the Tamudensis fort, continued the cult (or worship), which we will continue to observe from the 3rd day before the Ides of April in the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus and thereafter.
EDCS-05000871.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter for the welfare of the imperial family, including the Severan emperors and their mother Julia Domna, made by a military officer in Tamuda, dated to the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus (200 AD).

EDCS-05000874.text_edition: \n Caio Hosidio Cnaei filio Claudia Severo praefecto fabrum praefecto cohortis I Bosphoranorum praefecto cohortis IIII Raetorum tribuno militum legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis praefecto equitum alae Claudiae novae procuratori Augusti ad census in Brittannia Lucius Marius Luci filius Claudia Rufus Severianus consobrino optimo\n
EDCS-05000874.translation: To Gaius Hosidius Severus, son of Gnaeus, to his most excellent cousin, who was prefect of the artisans, prefect of the I Cohort of Bosphorani, prefect of the IIII Cohort of Raeti, military tribune of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, prefect of the horse of the Claudian Novae cavalry, and procurator of Augustus for the census in Britannia, Lucius Marius Rufus Severianus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-05000874.summary: This is an epitaph or dedicatory inscription for Gaius Hosidius Severus, detailing his distinguished military and administrative career under the Roman Empire, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07400770.text_edition: \n Genio municipii sancto Iulius Agriianus sic vir egregius procurator Augusti nostri pro legato\n
EDCS-07400770.translation: To the holy Genius of the municipality, Julius Agrippianus, a most distinguished man, the procurator of our Augustus, acting as legate.
EDCS-07400770.summary: This inscription honors the Genius of the municipality and is dedicated by Julius Agrippianus, who served as a procurator and pro-legate, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07400794.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caio Oppellio sic Antonino Diadumeniano Caesari principi iuventutis Imperatoris Marci Opelli Severi Macrini Augusti filio colonia Constantia devotissima numini eius ex decreto ordinis\n
EDCS-07400794.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Oppellius Antoninus Diadumenianus Caesar, prince of the youth, son of Emperor Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Augustus; the very devoted colony of Constantia erected this to his divine power by decree of the council.
EDCS-07400794.summary: This is a dedication by the colony of Constantia to Emperor Diadumenianus, son of Macrinus, by decree of the council, likely dating to 218 AD.

EDCS-07400795.text_edition: \n Iuliae Soaemiade sic Bassianae Augustae matri domini nostri colonia Iulia Constantia Zilil decreto ordinis decurionum\n
EDCS-07400795.translation: To Julia Soaemias Bassiana Augusta, mother of our lord; the colony of Iulia Constantia Ziza erected this by decree of the council of decurions.
EDCS-07400795.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colony of Ziza to Julia Soaemias, mother of Emperor Elagabalus, by decree of the local council, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08500654.text_edition: \n Valeri Vettonis militis cohortis I Lamavorum sic centuria Lucani hic situs est faciendum curavit Caius Flavius Pudens\n
EDCS-08500654.translation: Valerius Vettonis, a soldier of the I Cohort of Lamavorum, [was buried] in the century of Lucanus. Caius Flavius Pudens took care to have this made.
EDCS-08500654.summary: This is a tombstone for Valerius Vettonis, a soldier of the I Cohort of Lamavorum, who was buried in the century of Lucanus, and the monument was arranged by Caius Flavius Pudens, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800009.text_edition: \n Aurelia Sabina ancilla Cresti vixsit sic plus minus annis XXIII mensibus V diebus XIII horis VIIII fedelis sic in pace requet sic Amantio et Albino consolibus sic\n
EDCS-08800009.translation: Aurelia Sabina, a handmaid of Christ, lived more or less 23 years, 5 months, 13 days, 9 hours. Faithful, may she rest in peace. In the consulship of Amantius and Albinus.
EDCS-08800009.summary: This is an epitaph for Aurelia Sabina, a Christian woman, recording her age at death and the consuls of her burial year, Amantius and Albinus (likely late 4th or early 5th century AD).

EDCS-08800044.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-08800044.text_edition: \n g statim ut provinciam introivit sic barbaros qui Tamudam inruperant fugavit et in pacem restituit Victoriae Augustae sacrum\n
EDCS-08800044.translation: As soon as he entered the province, he routed the barbarians who had invaded Tamuda and restored them to peace. Sacred to Victory Augusta.
EDCS-08800044.summary: This inscription records a military victory, where an unnamed individual routed invading barbarians in Tamuda and restored peace, likely dedicated to Victoria Augusta, and probably from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800078.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patri patriae templum Matri Deum ex decreto ordinis Tiberi Claudii Iulianus et Saturninus duumviri fratres a solo faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08800078.translation: For the welfare of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland; Tiberius Claudius Iulianus and Saturninus, brothers, duumvirs, took care to build the temple to the Mother of the Gods from the ground up, by decree of the council.
EDCS-08800078.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to the Mother of the Gods for the welfare of Emperor Antoninus Pius, undertaken by two duumvirs, Tiberius Claudius Iulianus and Saturninus, by decree of the council, likely from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-08800079.text_edition: \n Exemplum epistulae Imperatorum nostrorum Antonini et Veri Augustorum ad Coiiedium Maximum liibellum Iuliani Zegrensis litteris tuis iunctum legimus et quamquam civitas Romana non nisi maximis meritis provocata indulgentia principali gentilibus istis dari solita sit tamen cum eum adfirmes et de primoribus esse popularium suorum et nostris rebus prompto obsequio fidissimum nec multas familias arbitraremur aput sic Zegrenses paria posse de officis suis praedicare quamquam plurimos cupiamus honore a nobis in istam domum conlato ad aemulationem Iuliani excitari non cunctamur et ipsi Ziddinae uxori item liberis Iuliano Maximo Maximino Diogeniano civitatem Romanam salvo iure gentis dare exemplum epistulae Imperatorum Antonini et Commodi Auggustorum ad Vallium Maximianum legimus libellum principis gentium Zegrensium animadvertimusque quali favore Epidi Quadrati praecessoris tui iuvetur proinde et illius testimonio et ipsius meritis et exemplis quae allegat permoti uxori filiisque eius civitatem Romanam salvo iure gentis dedimus quod in commentarios nostros referri possit explora quae cuiiusque aetatis sit et scribe nobis descriptum et recognitum ex commmentario civitate Romana donatorum divi Augusti et Tiberi Caesaris Augusti et Cai Caesaris et divi Claudi et Neronis et Galbae et divorum Augustorum Vespasiani et Titi et Caesaris Domitiani et divorum Augustorum Nervae et Traiiani Parthici et Traiiani Hadriani et Hadriani Antonini Pii et Veri Germanici Medici Parthici maximi et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Germanici Sarmatici et Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Aureli Commodi Augusti Germanici Sarmatici quem protulit Asclepiodotus libertus id quod infra scriptum est Imperatore Caesare Lucio Aurelio Commodo Augusto et Marco Plautio Quintilio consulibus pridie Nonas Iulias Romae Faggura uxor Iuliani principis gentis Zegrensium annos XXII Iuliana nnorum VIII Maxima nnorum III Iulianus nnorum III Diogenianus nnorum II liberi Iuliani supra scripti rogatu Aureli Iuliani principis Zegrensium per libellum suffragante Vallio Maximiano per epistulam his civitatem Romanam dedimus salvo iure gentis sine diminutione tributorum et vectigalium populi et fisci actum eodem die ibi isdem consulibus Asclepiodotus libertus recognovi signaverunt Marcus Gavius Marci filius Poblilia Squilla Gallicanus Manius Acilius Mani filius Galeria Glabrio Titus Sextius Titi filius Voturia Lateranus Caius Septimius Cai filius Quirina Severus Publius Iulius Cai filius Sergia Scapula Tertullus Titus Varius Titi filius Claudia Clemens Marcus Bassaeus Marci filius Stellatina Rufus Publius Taruttienus Publi filius Poblilia Paternus Sextus Tigidius filius Perennis Quintus Cervidius Quinti filius Arnensis Scaevola Quintus Larcius Quinti filius Quirina Euripianus Titus Flavius Titi filius Palatina Piso\n

EDCS-08800084.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Severi Pii Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Britannici maximi filius divi Marci Antonini Germanici Sarmatici nepos divi Antonini Pii pronepos divi Hadriani adnepos divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepos Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus Germanicus maximus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis XVIIII imperator III consul IIII pater patriae proconsul dicit obsequium et fidem vestram remunerans omnia quaecumque sunt debita fiscalia frumentaria sive pecuniaria pendentium quoque causarum concedo vobis exceptis de quibus pronuntiatum est provocatione non secuta et hoc amplius eas quoque causas at sic beneficium meum profiteor ipse pertinere in quibus appellationem interpositam probatum fuerit etiam si non sit admissa certum habens quod indulgentiam meam obsequio sitis remuneraturi cum vicorum et provinciarum bene de re publica merentium non tantum viris fortibus in omni ordine spectatissimis castrensium adque sic civilium officiorum verum etiam silvis quoque ipsis caelestium fertilibus animalium meritum aput sic me conlocaveritis hoc beneficio meo praesumo omnes de cetero annuas pensitationes sive in frumento seu in pecunia eo promptius daturos quo me reputabitis non expectasse quin ultro offerrem neque petentibus vobis neque sperantibus nova remedia et magnificam indulgentiam curantibus Lucio Antonio Sosibiano et Aulo Pompeio Cassiano duumviris\n

EDCS-08800088.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli proconsuli res publica Banasitana devota numini maiestatique eius\n

EDCS-08800090.text_edition: \n Ulpiae Severinae Augustae coniugi Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Domiti Aureliani Pii Felicis Augusti res publica Banasitana devota numini maiestatique eorum posuit\n

EDCS-08800092.text_edition: \n Flavius Ingenianus vir egregius procurator Augusti nostri devotus numini maiiestatique! eius\n

EDCS-08800109.text_edition: \n Quinto Iunio Rustico II Lucio Titio Plautio Aquilino consulibus Kalendas Februaris sic Aurelii Banasitani ex decreto splendidissimi ordinis Quintum Claudium Ferocem Quinti filium Aeronium Montanum patronum sibi liberisque ac posteris suis cooptaverunt Quintus Claudius Ferox Quinti filius Aeronius Montanus item patrocinium in se recepit egerunt legati Marcus Domitius Tingitanus IIvir Sextus Saenius Caecilianus Lucius Flavius Saturninus Decimus Iuventius Saturninus Quintus Iulius Martialis\n

EDCS-08800110.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Vespasiano Augusto VI Tito Imperatoris Augusti filio IIII consulibus Sextum Sentium Sexti filium Quirina Caecilianum legatum Augusti pro praetore ordinandae utriusqe Mauretaniae consulem designatum coloni coloniae Iuliae Valentiae Banasae ex provincia nova Mauretania Africa sibi liberis posterisque eorum patronum cooptaverunt Sextus Sentius Sexti filius Quirina Caecilianus legatus Augusti pro praetore ordinandae utriusque Mauretaniae consul designatus colonos coloniae Iuliae Valentiae Banasae ex provincia nova Mauretania ipsos liberos posterosque eorum in fidem clientelamque suam suorumque recipit egerunt legati Lucius Caecilius Quinti filius Fabia Calvus Lucius Sallustius Luci filius Fabia Senex filius\n

EDCS-08800112.text_edition: \n Lucius Labienus Luci filius Pollia colonos coloniae Iuliae Valentiae Banasae ipsos liberos posterosque eorum in fidem clientelamque suam liberorum posterorumque suorum recepit egerunt legati Caius Iulius Senis filius Fabia Rogatus Caius Pomponius Publi filius Fabia Masculus\n

EDCS-08800116.text_edition: \n Lucio Egnatio Luci filio Fabia Octaviano huic res publica Banasitana laudationem publicam et locum in foro dedit Aurelia Secunda mater honore usa filio piientissimo sic statuam equestrem posuit\n
EDCS-08800116.translation: The state of Banasa gave a public commendation and a place in the forum to Lucius Egnatius Octavianus, son of Lucius of the Fabian tribe; Aurelia Secunda, his mother, exercising her privilege, erected this equestrian statue to her most devoted son.
EDCS-08800116.summary: This inscription records the erection of an equestrian statue by a mother in honor of her son, Lucius Egnatius Octavianus, who received public honors from the community of Banasa, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08800267.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia, the Mede, the greatest Parthian, chief priest, in his seventh tribunician power, emperor for the fifth time, consul for the third time.
EDCS-08800267.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus, listing his titles and honors, including his victories in Armenia and Parthia, likely dating to the period of his co-rule with Marcus Aurelius (161-169 CE).

EDCS-08800269.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino maximo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto res publica Salensium decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08800269.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus maximus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, the state of Salensa by decree of its decurions.
EDCS-08800269.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the community of Salensa to Emperor Constantine the Great, honoring him with numerous epithets, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-08800275.text_edition: \n Quinto Antonio Cai filio Claudia Tranquillo Saturnino nnum quinque decurioni municipii Volubilitani et Salenses Antonius Priscus pater et Claudia Caeciliana mater\n
EDCS-08800275.translation: To Quintus Antonius Tranquillus Saturninus, son of Caius of the Claudia tribe, decurion of the municipium of Volubilis, his father Antonius Priscus and his mother Claudia Caeciliana [erected this] for five years.
EDCS-08800275.summary: This inscription records the dedication by parents, Antonius Priscus and Claudia Caeciliana, to their son Quintus Antonius Tranquillus Saturninus, who was a decurion in Volubilis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08800280.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cornelius Domitianus vixit annos Co[rnelius ------\n
EDCS-08800280.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Cornelius Domitianus lived for... Cornelius...
EDCS-08800280.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Cornelius Domitianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, recording his name and age at death, with the remainder of the inscription lost.

EDCS-08800311.text_edition: \n Genio Imperatoris [Luci Aureli Commodi] Augusti Sarmatici Germanici principis iuventutis Decimus Veturius Macrinus procurator Augusti conlocutus cum Canarta principe constituto genti sic Baquatium III Idus Octobres Praesente II et Condiano consulibus\n
EDCS-08800311.translation: To the Genius of the Emperor Lucius Aurelius Commodus Augustus Sarmaticus Germanicus, Prince of Youth. Decimus Veturius Macrinus, procurator of the Emperor, having conferred with Canarta, the prince, established for the Baquatium tribe on the 3rd day before the Ides of October, with Praesens and Condianus as consuls.
EDCS-08800311.summary: This inscription records an agreement made by the imperial procurator Decimus Veturius Macrinus with Canarta, prince of the Baquatium tribe, honoring Emperor Commodus, likely in 179 CE.

EDCS-08800312.text_edition: \n Genio Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis et Marci Aureli Antonini et Publi Septimi Getae Caesaris Auggustorum Caius Sertorius Cattianus procurator eorum conlocutus cum Ililasene principe gentis Baquatium filio Ureti principis gentis eiusdem pridie Nonas Martias Victorino et Proculo consulibus\n
EDCS-08800312.translation: To the Genius of the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar Augusti. Caius Sertorius Cattianus, their procurator, having conferred with Ililasene, son of Ureti, prince of the same tribe of the Baquatium, on the day before the Nones of March, with Victorinus and Proculus as consuls.
EDCS-08800312.summary: This inscription records an agreement made by the imperial procurator Caius Sertorius Cattianus with Ililasene, prince of the Baquatium tribe, concerning the emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, likely in 205 CE.

EDCS-08800316.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ob vota decennalia celebrata et vicenalia suscepta Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi Pii Felicis et Marci Aureli Antonini Auggustorum [[]] s totiusque domus divinae Gnaeus Haius Diadumenianus proconsul Auggustorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08800316.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the celebrated tenth anniversary and undertaken twentieth anniversary vows of the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Felix and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti, and of the entire divine house. Gnaeus Haius Diadumenianus, proconsul of the Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08800316.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of vows for the tenth and twentieth anniversaries of the emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla by the proconsul Gnaeus Haius Diadumenianus, likely around 204 CE.

EDCS-08800321.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis deabusque immortalibus pro salute et incolumitate et victoriae sic Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Iulii Philippi Pii Felicis Augusti et Marci Iuli Philippi nobilissimi Caesaris et Marciae Otaciliae Severae coniugis Augusti nostri et matris Caesaris nostri et matris castrorum et senatus Marcus Maturius Victorinus procurator eorum pro legato conloquium sic cum Sepemazine principe gentis Baquatium pacis confirmandae gratia aramque consecravit X Kalendas Maias Imperatore domino nostro Marco Iulio Philippo et Messio Tetiano sic consulibus\n
EDCS-08800321.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all the other immortal gods and goddesses, for the safety, well-being, and victory of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, and Marcus Julius Philippus, the most noble Caesar, and Marcia Otacilia Severa, wife of our Augustus, and mother of our Caesar, and mother of the camp, and of the senate. Marcus Maturius Victorinus, their procurator, acting as legate, having conferred with Sepemazine, prince of the Baquatium tribe, consecrated this altar for the confirmation of peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of May, with our lord Marcus Julius Philippus and Messius Tetianus as consuls.
EDCS-08800321.summary: This inscription records the consecration of an altar by the imperial procurator Marcus Maturius Victorinus to Jupiter and other gods for the well-being and victory of Emperor Philip the Arab and his family, likely in 245 CE.

EDCS-08800322.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Genio et Bonae Fortunae Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Probi Invicti Augusti nostri Clementius Valerius Marcellinus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Mauretaniae Tingitanae conloquio habito cum Iulio Nuffuzi filio Iuli Matif regis gentis Baquatium foederatae pacis aram statuit et dedicavit die VIIII Kalendas Novembres domino nostro Probo Augusto et Paulino consulibus\n
EDCS-08800322.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Genius, and the Good Fortune of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Invictus Augustus. Clementius Valerius Marcellinus, most perfect man, governor of the province of Mauretania Tingitana, having held a conference with Julius Nuffuzius, son of Juli Matif, king of the allied Baquatium tribe, established and dedicated this altar of allied peace on the 9th day before the Kalends of November, with our lord Probus Augustus and Paulinus as consuls.
EDCS-08800322.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar of peace by the governor Clementius Valerius Marcellinus to Jupiter, the Genius, and the Good Fortune of Emperor Probus, following a conference with the king of the Baquatium tribe, likely in 277 CE.

EDCS-08800323.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo diis deabusque immortalibus et Genio Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli CASERNAV A[ugusti] ob diutinam pacem servatam cum Iulio Nuffusi et nunc conloquio habito cum Iulio Mirzi fratre eiiusdem sic Nuffusis principis gentis Ba<q=O>uatium Clementius Valerius Marcellinus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Mauretaniae Tingitanae confirmata pace aram posuit et dedicavit Idibus Aprilibus Messala et Grato consulibus \n
EDCS-08800323.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the immortal gods and goddesses, and to the Genius of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius, son of the Divine Hadrian, Augustus, for the long-maintained peace preserved with Julius Nuffusius and now after a conference held with Julius Mirzius, his brother, and Nuffusius, prince of the Baquates tribe, Clementius Valerius Marcellinus, a most distinguished man and governor of the province of Mauretania Tingitana, having confirmed the peace, erected and dedicated this altar on the Ides of April, when Messala and Gratus were consuls.
EDCS-08800323.summary: This inscription records the erection and dedication of an altar by the governor of Mauretania Tingitana to Roman deities and the Emperor's Genius, commemorating the maintenance of peace with local tribal leaders, dated to 166 CE.

EDCS-08800325.text_edition: \n Pro salute et incolumitate Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Aeli Aureli Commodi Pii Invicti Felicis Herculis Romani imperioque eius Aurelius Nectoreca |(centurio) vexillariorum Brittannicianorum Volubili agentium sua pecunia Invicto posuit et dedicavit\n
EDCS-08800325.translation: For the health and safety of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Pius Felix Invictus, the Hero of Rome, and for his empire, Aurelius Nectoreca, a centurion of the vexillations from Britannia stationed at Volubilis, erected and dedicated this to the Invictus at his own expense.
EDCS-08800325.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by a centurion of auxiliary troops for the health of Emperor Commodus, erected at his own expense in Volubilis, likely dating to Commodus's reign (177-192 CE).

EDCS-08800331.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Caesari Augusto divi sic filio Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate IIII consuli III designato IIII imperatori VIII patri patriae municipium Volubilitanorum impetrata civitate Romana et conubio et oneribus remissis decreto decurionum dedit Marcus Fadius Celer Flavianus Maximus procurator Augusti pro legato dedicavit\n
EDCS-08800331.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of the Divine Claudius, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, consul for the 3rd time, commander for the 8th time, father of the country, the municipality of Volubilitani, having obtained Roman citizenship, the right of marriage, and relief from burdens, by decree of the decurions, gave [this monument]; Marcus Fadius Celer Flavianus Maximus, procurator of the Emperor, acting as legate, dedicated it.
EDCS-08800331.summary: This inscription records the granting of Roman citizenship and other privileges to the municipality of Volubilis by Emperor Claudius, and the subsequent dedication of a monument by the municipal authorities and the imperial procurator, dating to Claudius's reign (41-54 CE).

EDCS-08800332.translation: To the Divine Claudius, by the Volubilitani, who were granted Roman citizenship by him.
EDCS-08800332.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Emperor Claudius by the people of Volubilis, likely commemorating the granting of Roman citizenship, dating to the period after his deification.

EDCS-08800336.text_edition: \n Divae Matidiae municipium Volubilitanum decreto decurionum posuit\n
EDCS-08800336.translation: To the Divine Matidia, the municipality of Volubilis erected [this] by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08800336.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by the municipality of Volubilis to the deified Matidia, by decree of its town council, likely dating to the period after her deification.

EDCS-08800338.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani fili divi Traiiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli III patri patriae Aelius Tuccuda princeps gentis Baquatium\n
EDCS-08800338.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the Divine Hadrian, grandson of the Divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the Divine Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, Aelius Tuccuda, prince of the Baquates tribe.
EDCS-08800338.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Aelius Tuccuda, a prince of the Baquates tribe, to Emperor Antoninus Pius, listing the emperor's full titulature and divine ancestry, likely dating to Antoninus Pius's reign (138-161 CE).

EDCS-08800340.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Augusto divo Antonino Pio Lucius Annius Matun Anni Honorati libertus ob honorem IIIIIIviratus posuit dedicavitque\n
EDCS-08800340.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Augustus, the Divine Antoninus Pius, Lucius Annius Matun, freedman of Annius Honoratus, erected and dedicated [this] on account of the honor of the office of duovir for the fourth time.
EDCS-08800340.summary: This inscription records the erection and dedication of a monument by a freedman to the deified Emperor Antoninus Pius, in honor of his holding the office of duovir, likely dating to the period after Antoninus Pius's reign.

EDCS-08800341.text_edition: \n Divae Faustinae Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae consulis III Volubilitani publice posuerunt\n
EDCS-08800341.translation: To the Divine Faustina, wife of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, consul for the 3rd time, the people of Volubilis publicly erected [this].
EDCS-08800341.summary: This inscription records the public erection of a monument by the people of Volubilis to the deified Empress Faustina, wife of Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely dating to the period after her deification.

EDCS-08800345.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Armeniaci Medici Parthici Germanici maximi Epidius Quadratus procurator eius conlocutus cum Ucmetio principe gentium Macennitum et Baquatium\n
EDCS-08800345.translation: For the health of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus, Medicus, Parthicus, Germanicus Maximus, Epidius Quadratus, his procurator, conferred with Ucmetius, prince of the Macennites and Baquates tribes.
EDCS-08800345.summary: This inscription records a procurator of Emperor Marcus Aurelius conferring with local tribal princes regarding the emperor's health, likely dating to Marcus Aurelius's reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-08800346.translation: To the Divine Faustina Augusta, wife of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-08800346.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Empress Faustina Augusta, wife of Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely dating to the period after her deification.

EDCS-08800348.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Marci Antonini Germanici Sarmatici filio divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiiani sic Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Parthico Arabico et Parthico Adiabenico patri patriae pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate IIII imperatori VIII consuli II proconsuli et Marco Aurelio Antonino Caesari et Iuliae Domnae Augustae matri castrorum res publica Volubilitanorum ex decreto ordinis posuit\n
EDCS-08800348.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, father of the fatherland, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 4th time, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, and to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, and to Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the camps, the Republic of Volubilitanus erected this by decree of its council.
EDCS-08800348.summary: This inscription records a statue base dedicated by the city of Volubilitanus to Emperor Septimius Severus and his family, likely dating to his fourth tribunician power.

EDCS-08800350.translation: [This inscription is too fragmentary to translate.]
EDCS-08800350.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription mentioning Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely an emperor, but the rest is illegible.

EDCS-08800358.translation: To Emperor Caesar Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, proconsul, the Republic of Volubilitanus erected this by decree of its council.
EDCS-08800358.summary: This inscription records a statue base erected by the city of Volubilitanus to Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-08800361.text_edition: \n Anniae Faustinae Augustae coniugi Augusti nostri et matri Caesaris nostri res publica Volubilitanorum devotissima numini eorum ex decreto ordinis posuit\n
EDCS-08800361.translation: The Republic of Volubilitanus, most devoted to their divine will, erected this by decree of its council to Annia Faustina Augusta, wife of our Augustus and mother of our Caesar.
EDCS-08800361.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by the city of Volubilitanus to Annia Faustina, wife of the emperor and mother of Caesar.

EDCS-08800362.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Magni Antonini filio divi Pii Severi nepoti Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli res publica Volubilitanorum ex decreto ordinis posuit\n
EDCS-08800362.translation: The Republic of Volubilitanus erected this by decree of its council to Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Magnus, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul.
EDCS-08800362.summary: This inscription records a statue base erected by the city of Volubilitanus to Emperor Severus Alexander, likely dating to his consulship.

EDCS-08800364.translation: The Republic of Volubilitanus, most devoted to her divine will, erected this by decree of its council to Julia Mamaea Pius Felix Augusta, mother of our Augustus.
EDCS-08800364.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by the city of Volubilitanus to Julia Mamaea, mother of the reigning emperor.

EDCS-08800365.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus restored this house with a bathhouse, which had collapsed from age, from the ground, under the supervision of Marcus Ulpius Victor, a distinguished man, procurator, acting as governor.
EDCS-08800365.summary: This inscription records Emperor Gordian III's restoration of a public building containing a bathhouse in Volubilitanus, overseen by his procurator.

EDCS-08800366.translation: The Republic of Volubilitanus erected this by decree of its council to the Emperor Caesar [Publius] Licinius Gallienus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, father of the fatherland, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul.
EDCS-08800366.summary: This inscription records a statue base erected by the city of Volubilitanus to Emperor Gallienus, likely dating to his third tribunician power and second consulship.

EDCS-08800368.translation: The Republic of Volubilitanus erected this by decree of its council to Emperor Caesar [Marcus Aurelius] Claudius Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-08800368.summary: This inscription records a statue base erected by the city of Volubilitanus to Emperor Claudius Gothicus.

EDCS-08800369.translation: The Republic of Volubilitanus erected this by decree of its council to Ulpia Severina Augusta, wife of our lord Aurelianus Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-08800369.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by the city of Volubilitanus to Empress Ulpia Severina, wife of Emperor Aurelian.

EDCS-08800389.text_edition: \n Lucio Pompeio Marci filio Claudia Seniori Volubili tribuno militum legionis VIII Augustae praefecto cohortis II Flaviae Afrorum equo publico exornato a divo Pio decurioni municipi sui Pompeii Antonianus et Manlianus fili posuerunt\n
EDCS-08800389.translation: To Lucius Pompeius, son of Marcus, Claudia Senior, known as Volubilis, tribune of soldiers of the Eighth Augustan Legion, prefect of the Second Cohort of Flavians of Africa, honored with the equestrian rank by the divine Pius. Pompeius Antonianus and Manlianus, his sons, erected this.
EDCS-08800389.summary: A funerary monument erected by sons for their father, Lucius Pompeius Volubilis, a military officer, likely from Volubilis, dated perhaps to the Antonine period.

EDCS-08800391.text_edition: \n Aemiliae Decimi filiae Sextinae Viennensi bis flaminicae ordo Volubilitanorum ob eximiam eius probitatem et mariti sui Nammi Materni praefecti cohortis Asturum et Callaecorum merita locum sepulchri inpensam funeris statuam decrevit Nammius Maternus contentus honore inpensam remisit sua pecunia posuit\n
EDCS-08800391.translation: To Aemilia Sextina, daughter of Decimus, of Vienna, twice priestess of the imperial cult. The town council of Volubilis decreed for her the place of burial, the expenses of the funeral, and a statue, on account of her exceptional integrity and the merits of her husband, Nammius Maternus, prefect of the Cohort of Asturians and Callaecians. Nammius Maternus, content with the honor, remitted the expenses and erected this at his own expense.
EDCS-08800391.summary: A funerary honor granted by the town council of Volubilis to Aemilia Sextina, recognizing her virtue and her husband's military service, with the husband covering the costs.

EDCS-08800403.text_edition: \n Marco Valerio Bostaris filio Galeria Severo aedili sufeti IIviro flamini primo in municipio suo praefecto auxiliorum adversus Aedemonem oppressum bello huic ordo municipii Volubilitanorum ob merita erga rem publicam et legationem bene gestam qua ab divo Claudio civitatem Romanam et conubium cum peregrinis mulieribus immunitatem nnum X incolas sic bona civium bello interfectorum quorum heredes non extabant suis impetravit Fabia Bira Izeltae filia uxor indulgentissimo viro honore usa impensam remisit et de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08800403.translation: To Marcus Valerius Severus, son of Bostaris, of the Galerian tribe, aedile, two-time sufes, flamen primus in his town, prefect of auxiliaries against Aedemon, defeated in war. The town council of Volubilis granted this man, on account of his merits towards the state and his well-conducted legation, by which he obtained Roman citizenship and the right of intermarriage with foreign women, and immunity for ten years for the inhabitants, and also the property of citizens killed in war whose heirs did not exist, for himself. Fabia Bira, daughter of Izelta, his wife, using the honor granted to her most indulgent husband, remitted the expenses and dedicated this from her own money.
EDCS-08800403.summary: A funerary monument honoring Marcus Valerius Severus for his public service and diplomatic achievements, including securing Roman citizenship and property for citizens, erected by his wife.

EDCS-08800434.translation: L(ucius) Caecilius [son of L(ucius)?], of the Quirina tribe. L(ucius) Iulius, son of L(ucius), of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-08800434.summary: Fragmentary inscription likely recording the names and tribal affiliations of two Roman citizens, possibly related to a funerary or public monument.

EDCS-08800719.text_edition: \n Salvo et Invicto domino nostro Seve[ro] Alexandro Pio Felici Augusto balneum vetus vetustate conlapsum a fundamentis restituit exampliata priore cella Lucius Aurelius Nemesianus vir egregius procurator Augusti nostri curante Tiberio Claudio Zenone tribuno cohortis IIII miliariae Tungrorum\n
EDCS-08800719.translation: To our lord Salvo and Invicto, Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus. The old bath, having collapsed from age, was restored from its foundations and the previous cell duplicated by Lucius Aurelius Nemesianus, an excellent man, procurator of our Augustus, under the supervision of Tiberius Claudius Zeno, tribune of the Fourth Cohort of Tungri, equipped with light arms.
EDCS-08800719.summary: A dedication recording the restoration of a public bathhouse, commissioned by the procurator Lucius Aurelius Nemesianus and supervised by the tribune Tiberius Claudius Zeno, likely during the reign of Severus Alexander.

EDCS-12100793.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus \n
EDCS-12100793.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the divine Nerva, greatest pontiff.
EDCS-12100793.summary: The beginning of an inscription or titulature, likely from the reign of Emperor Trajan, listing his key titles and divine parentage.

EDCS-12300363.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300363.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Vespasiani filius Domitianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator XIIII censor perpetuus consul XIIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militant in alis quinque quae appellantur I Augusta et I Hamiorum et III Asturum et Gemelliana et Tauriana et cohortibus duabus II milliaria sagittariorum et V Delmatarum item dimissis honesta missione ex iisdem alis et cohortibus quinque I Lemavorum et I Bracarorum et II milliaria sagittaria et IIII Gallorum et V Delmatarum quae sunt in Mauretania Tingitana sub Lucio Vallio Tranquillo qui quina et vicena plurave stipendia meruerunt quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem V Idus Ianuarias Imperatore Caesare Domitiano Augusto Germanico XIIII Lucio Minicio Rufo consulibus cohortis II milliariae sagittariae cui praeest Tiberius Claudius Pedo equiti Domitio Domiti filio Philad descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in Capitolio in tabulario publico parte sinisteriore Imperator Caesar divi Vespasiani filius Domitianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator XIIII censor perpetuus consul XIIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militant in alis quinque quae appellantur I Augusta et I Hamiorum et III Asturum et Gemelliana et Tauriana et cohortibus duabus II milliaria sagittariorum et V Delmatarum item dimissis honesta missione ex iisdem alis et cohortibus quinque I Lemavorum et I Bracarorum et II milliaria sagittaria et IIII Gallorum et V Delmatarum quae sunt in Maur<e=F>tania Tingitana sub Lucio Vallio Tran quillo qui quina et vicena plurave stipendia meruerunt quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis pos<t=I>(e)risque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem V Idus Ianuarias Imperatore Caesare Domitiano Augusto G<e=C>rmanico XIIII Lucio Minicio Rufo consulibus cohortis II milliariae sagittariae cui praeest Tiberius Claudius Pedo equiti Domitio Domi<t=I>i filio Philad descriptum et reco<g=C>nitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in Capitolio Cai Curti Nigri Marci Egnati Rufi Cai Lucreti Modesti Luci Arri Iusti Publi Petroni Pavill<i=T> Titi Iuli Frontonis Luci Clevani Firmi \n
EDCS-12300363.translation: Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Vespasian, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 7th time, imperator for the 14th time, perpetual censor, consul for the 14th time, father of the country, granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the cavalrymen and infantrymen serving in five alae, called I Augusta, I Hamiorum, III Asturum, Gemelliana, and Tauriana, and in two cohorts, II milliaria sagittariorum and V Delmatarum, and also to those honorably discharged from the same alae and five cohorts (I Lemavorum, I Bracarorum, II milliaria sagittaria, IIII Gallorum, and V Delmatarum) which are in Mauretania Tingitana under Lucius Vallius Tranquillus, who have completed twenty or more years of service, whose names are listed below, to them, their children, and descendants. This was granted on the fifth day before the Ides of January, when Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus was consul for the 14th time with Lucius Minicius Rufus. For the infantryman of the II milliaria sagittaria cohort, commanded by Tiberius Claudius Pedo, to Domitius, son of Domitius, of the Philades tribe, it is inscribed and recognized from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitol in the public archives on the left side. Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Vespasian, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 7th time, imperator for the 14th time, perpetual censor, consul for the 14th time, father of the country, granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the cavalrymen and infantrymen serving in five alae, called I Augusta, I Hamiorum, III Asturum, Gemelliana, and Tauriana, and in two cohorts, II milliaria sagittariorum and V Delmatarum, and also to those honorably discharged from the same alae and five cohorts (I Lemavorum, I Bracarorum, II milliaria sagittaria, IIII Gallorum, and V Delmatarum) which are in Mauretania Tingitana under Lucius Vallius Tranquillus, who have completed twenty or more years of service, whose names are listed below, to them, their children, and descendants. This was granted on the fifth day before the Ides of January, when Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus was consul for the 14th time with Lucius Minicius Rufus. For the infantryman of the II milliaria sagittaria cohort, commanded by Tiberius Claudius Pedo, to Domitius, son of Domitius, of the Philades tribe, it is inscribed and recognized from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitol by Caius Curtius Niger, Marcus Egnatius Rufus, Caius Lucretius Modestus, Lucius Arrius Justus, Publius Petronius Pavillus, Titus Julius Fronton, Lucius Clevanius Firmus.
EDCS-12300363.summary: An imperial grant of citizenship and marriage rights to auxiliary soldiers serving in Mauretania Tingitana, issued by Emperor Domitian and recorded on a bronze tablet in Rome, listing the specific legions and consuls of the period.

EDCS-12300365.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300365.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIII imperator VI consul V pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis duabus et cohortibus sex quae appellantur I Hamiorum sagittariorum et III Asturum piae fidelis civium Romanorum et I Ituraeorum civium Romanorum et I Lemavorum civium Romanorum et II Hispanorum civium Romanorum et II Hispana civium Romanorum et IIII Gallorum civium Romanorum et V Delmatarum et sunt in Mauretania Tingitana sub Marco Clodio Catullo quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas pridie Idus Octobres Caio Iulio Proculo Caio Aburnio Valente consulibus alae I Hamiorum sagittariorum cui praeest Caius Maesius Cai filius Palatina Tertius ex gregale Bargati Zaei filio Hamio et Iuliae Iuli filiae Deisatae uxoris eius Surae et Zenae filiae eius et Saturnino filio eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIII imperator VI consul V pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis duabus et cohortibus sex quae appellantur I Hamiorum sagittariorum et III Asturum piae fidelis civium Romanorum et I Ituraeorum civium Romanorum et I Lemavorum civium Romanorum et II Hispanorum civium Romanorum et II Hispana civium Romanorum et IIII Gallorum civium Romanorum et V Delmatarum et sunt in Mauretania Tingitana sub Marco Clodio Catullo quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas pridie Idus Octobres Caio Iulio Proculo Caio Aburnio Valente consulibus alae I Hamiorum sagittariorum cui praeest Caius Maesius Cai filius Palatina Tertius Publi Atini Amerimni Cai Iuli Parati Cai Tuticani Saturnini Marci Iuli Clementis\n
EDCS-12300365.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the divine Nerva, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 13th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the country, granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in two alae and six cohorts (called I Hamiorum sagittariorum, III Asturum pia fidelis civium Romanorum, I Ituraeorum civium Romanorum, I Lemavorum civium Romanorum, II Hispanorum civium Romanorum, II Hispana civium Romanorum, IIII Gallorum civium Romanorum, and V Delmatarum) and are in Mauretania Tingitana under Marcus Clodius Catullus, having completed twenty or more years of service, who were honorably discharged and whose names are listed below, to them, their children, and descendants. This was granted on the day before the Ides of October, when Caius Julius Proculus and Caius Aburnius Valentinus were consuls. For the ala I Hamiorum sagittariorum, commanded by Caius Maesius Tertius, son of Caius, of the Palatina tribe, to Bargatus, son of Zaeus, from the ranks of the Hamians, and to his wife Iulia Deisata, daughter of Julius, and their daughters Sura and Zena, and their son Saturninus. It is inscribed and recognized from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the wall behind the Temple of the Divine Augustus near Minerva. Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the divine Nerva, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 13th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the country, granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in two alae and six cohorts (called I Hamiorum sagittariorum, III Asturum pia fidelis civium Romanorum, I Ituraeorum civium Romanorum, I Lemavorum civium Romanorum, II Hispanorum civium Romanorum, II Hispana civium Romanorum, IIII Gallorum civium Romanorum, and V Delmatarum) and are in Mauretania Tingitana under Marcus Clodius Catullus, having completed twenty or more years of service, who were honorably discharged and whose names are listed below, to them, their children, and descendants. This was granted on the day before the Ides of October, when Caius Julius Proculus and Caius Aburnius Valentinus were consuls. For the ala I Hamiorum sagittariorum, commanded by Caius Maesius Tertius, son of Caius, of the Palatina tribe, to Publius Atinius Amerimnus, Caius Julius Paratus, Caius Tuticanus Saturninus, Marcus Julius Clemens.
EDCS-12300365.summary: An imperial grant of citizenship and marriage rights by Emperor Trajan to auxiliary soldiers in Mauretania Tingitana, detailing the units, commanders, consuls, and specific recipients, recorded from a bronze tablet.

EDCS-12800702.text_edition: \n ates Isidi votum dominae Isidi dimiserunt\n
EDCS-12800702.translation: To Isis, the Mistress, they sent their vow. They released [her?].
EDCS-12800702.summary: A short dedication to the goddess Isis, likely part of a larger votive inscription or offering.

EDCS-16201963.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marci Aureli Antonini Pii filio Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Germanico Sarmatico Arabico Adiabenico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III imperatori VII consuli II proconsuli colonia Banasitana decreto decurionum curantibus Domitio Seret Iulio GeminIIviris\n
EDCS-16201963.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Germanicus Sarmaticus Arabicus Adiabenicus, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, imperator for the 7th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul. The colony of Banasitana, by decree of the decurions, under the supervision of Domitius Seret and Julius Gemini, duovirs.
EDCS-16201963.summary: A dedication by the colony of Banasitana to Emperor Septimius Severus, listing his titles and the local magistrates, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-24700496.translation: To Titus Caesar, son of Emperor Vespasian.
EDCS-24700496.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Titus Caesar, son of Emperor Vespasian, likely part of a larger monument.

EDCS-24700497.translation: To Matidia Augusta. Antonia Suavis, her heir by will, set this up.
EDCS-24700497.summary: This inscription records the funerary dedication to Matidia Augusta by her heir, Antonia Suavis, from the Augustan period.

EDCS-24700498.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXI imperatori II consuli IIII patri patriae cultores domus Augustae area privatam emptam templum cum porticibus a solo sua pecunia fecerunt et statuam posuerunt quorum nomina tabulae aereae incisa sunt dedicante Quinto Aeronio Mon<t=I>ano procuratore \n
EDCS-24700498.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 21st time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland. The cultores of the imperial household, having purchased a private area, built a temple with porticoes from the ground up at their own expense and erected a statue, whose names are inscribed on bronze tablets, with Quintus Aerontius Montanus as procurator.
EDCS-24700498.summary: This is a dedication by the cultores domus Augustae to Emperor Antoninus Pius, recording the construction of a temple and the erection of a statue, dating to his reign (mid-2nd century AD).

EDCS-24700499.text_edition: \n Genio Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Publius Aelius Crispinus procurator conlocutus cum o principe gentium IIII IIIONPETM \n
EDCS-24700499.translation: To the Genius of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus. Publius Aelius Crispinus, procurator, conferred with the princeps gentium.
EDCS-24700499.summary: This inscription dedicates something to the Genius of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus by Publius Aelius Crispinus, a procurator, likely from the late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-24700501.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Veldumnianus Volusianus, Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul, proconsul. The republic of Volubilitanum, by decree of the council, set this up.
EDCS-24700501.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by the republic of Volubilitanum to Emperor Volusianus, dating to his brief reign in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-24700503.text_edition: \n Marco Antonio Quirina Navillo omnibus equestribus militiis functo Marcus Antonius Navillus Asiaticus filius vir egregius procurator Augusti patri karissimo\n
EDCS-24700503.translation: To Marcus Antonius Quirinus Navillus, who had held all equestrian offices. His son Marcus Antonius Navillus Asiaticus, a distinguished man and procurator of Augustus, to his dearest father.
EDCS-24700503.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Marcus Antonius Navillus Asiaticus for his father, Marcus Antonius Quirinus Navillus, a man who had held equestrian offices and served as a procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-24700516.text_edition: \n Marco Pompeio Luci filio Claudia Antoniano Volubilitano decurioni maunicipii sui Lucius Pompeius Luci filius Manlianus fratri indulgentissimo et optime de se merito sua pecunia posuit\n
EDCS-24700516.translation: To Marcus Pompeius Lucii filius Volubilitanus, decurion of his own municipality. Lucius Pompeius Lucii filius Manlianus, to his most affectionate and deserving brother, set this up at his own expense.
EDCS-24700516.summary: This funerary dedication was erected by Lucius Pompeius Manlianus for his brother Marcus Pompeius Volubilitanus, a decurion, likely from the Roman colony of Volubilis in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-25600691.text_edition: \n Publio Besio Publi filio Quirina Betuiniano Caio Mario Memmio Sabino praefecto cohortis I Raetorum tribuno legionis X geminae piae fidelis praefecto alae Dardanorum procuratori Imperatoris Caesaris Nervae Traiani Augusti Germanici Dacici monetae procuratori provinciae Baeticae procuratori XX hereditatium procuratori pro legato provinciae Mauretaniae Tingitanae donis donato ab Imperatore Traiano Augusto bello Dacico corona murali vallari hastis puris vexillo argenteo exacti exercitus\n
EDCS-25600691.translation: To Publius Besius Betuinianus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, who had served as tribune of the Cohors I Raetorum, tribune of the Legio X Gemina Pia Fidelis, prefect of the Ala Dardanorum, procurator of Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus of the mint, procurator of the province of Baetica, procurator of the 20 hereditates, governor of the province of Mauretania Tingitana, honored by Emperor Trajan Augustus in the Dacian War with a mural crown, a vallaris crown, pure javelins, and a silver vexillum, and commanded troops. [The inscription is incomplete.]
EDCS-25600691.summary: This extensive inscription lists the career and honors of Publius Besius Betuinianus, a Roman official who served in various military and administrative posts under Trajan, likely from the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-25600693.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Germanicus Sarmaticus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the 3rd time. Arranged by Gaius Castricius [---] II and Quintus Junius Ga[---] II, duovirs of the colony of Aurelia Banasa. A place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25600693.summary: This inscription records a public work in the colony of Aurelia Banasa, dedicated to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, dating to his consulship, likely in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-25601168.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, Minerva, and all the immortal gods and goddesses, for the safety of our lords Emperors Diocletian and Maximian, and Constantius and Maximian, the most noble Caesars. Frontonianus set this up under [---] in the consulship of Diocletian Augustus for the 6th time and Constantius Caesar for the second time.
EDCS-25601168.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the gods for the health of the co-emperors Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Maximian, placed by Frontonianus during their consulships, dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-25601169.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletianus Germanicus Maximus, Pious, Happy, Unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the eighth time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, proconsul.
EDCS-25601169.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Emperor Diocletian, listing his titles and honors, likely dating to his eighth tribunician power and fourth consulship.

EDCS-26801208.text_edition: \n ------ in]nocentiae et iustitiae singularis Aulo Caecinae Tacito consuli praesidi provinciae Baeticae VIIviro epulonum praetori candidato quaestori candidato Septimius Carvilianus eques Romanus patrono incomparabili\n
EDCS-26801208.translation: To Aulus Caecina Tacitus, consul, governor of the province of Baetica, VIIvir epulonum, candidate for praetor, candidate for quaestor, incomparable patron, for his singular innocence and justice, Septimius Carvilianus, a Roman equestrian, dedicated this.
EDCS-26801208.summary: This is a dedication to Aulus Caecina Tacitus, a provincial governor, by Septimius Carvilianus, an equestrian, likely dating to Tacitus's tenure as governor.

EDCS-33500235.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis immortalibus pro salute atque incolumitate ddominorum nnostrorum Diocletiani et Maximiani Augustorum Marcus Aurelius Cletus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Mauretaniae Tingitanae pro pace provinciae dedit dedicavit die XIII Kalendas Octobres Tiberiano II Dione consulibus\n
EDCS-33500235.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all the immortal gods, for the health and safety of our lords Diocletian and Maximian Augusti, Marcus Aurelius Cletus, a man of highest rank and governor of the province of Mauretania Tingitana, dedicated this for the peace of the province, on the thirteenth day before the Kalends of October, when Tiberianus II and Dionysius were consuls.
EDCS-33500235.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and other gods by Marcus Aurelius Cletus, governor of Mauretania Tingitana, for the safety of the emperors Diocletian and Maximian and the peace of the province, dated by the consuls Tiberianus II and Dionysius.

EDCS-57900121.inscription_type: senatus consulta, tituli honorarii, tituli operum
EDCS-57900121.text_edition: \n Marco Sulpicio Marci filio Felici domo Roma tribu Quirina liberatori et patrono praefecto cohortis I Germanorum tribuno militum legionis XVI Flaviae firmae fidelis tribuno militum cohortis III Ulpiae Mmiliariae Petraeorum electo et retento ad census excipiendos in partem provinciae Armeniae item Cappadociae praefecto equitum alae II Syrorum civium Romanorum amici ob adfectionem municipii Salensis et innocentiam dono dederunt decretumque ordinis subiecerunt Marcus Valerius Fabullus Lucius Fabius Vivatianus Marcus Antonius Bassianus Caius Valerius Saturninus Caius Fabius Fidus Lucius Fabius Fabianus Caius Cassius Saturninus Lucius Valerius Gallus Caius Valerius Rogatus Publius Postumius Hermesander Caius Antonius Priscus Caius Fabius Modestus Caius Valerius Avitus Marcus Valerius Capito Quintus Pontius Capitolinus Lucius Hortensius Maurus Quintus Fabius Pudens Marcus Iunius Cassianus Quintus Herennius Tuscus Quintus Fabius Fabricianus Quintus Valerius Martialis Marcus Fabius Virillio Caius Annius Capito Caius Iunius Cassianus Lucius Valerius Cornelius Saturninus Marcus Valerius Polio Quintus Iunius Rufinianus Sextus Antonius Bassinus Marcus Cassius Cassianus quaestor Titus Cassius Bassianus Titus Aelius Africanus Caius Atilius Zubbaeus Lucius Cornelius Victor Cnaeus Cornelius Cornelianus Cnaeus Valerius Primus Quintus Fabius Amandus Quintus Valerius Saturninus et Titus Aelius Zosimus Lollio Avito et Statilio Maxsimo sic consulibus V Kalendas Novembres in curia Ulpia adhibito Salensium ordine quot sic Caius Valerius Rogatus et Publius Postumius Hermesander IIviri rettulerunt optimo rarissimoque praefecto Sulpicio Felici successorem nominatum cui pro exsimis sic eius exsemplis sic cum et ante testimonium publice perhibitum sit nunc praecipuae sic manifestiore iudicio quam studio esse perhibendum quoniam oporteret simul nova totiens et in rem publicam merita et in singulos officia novis honoribus totiens rependere simul spem parem ostentare pariter acturis quit sic de ea re fieri placeret secundum sententiam Quinti Corneli Capellae cuncti censuerunt omnia a Sulpicio Felice optumo rarissimoque praefecto Salenses habere quibus meliora expetenda non fuerint si is patriae isti eodem hoc splendore civis contigisset qui pro caelestibus iudicis quibus intra iuventam per tres militias exornatus sit proque discipulina sic Uttedi Honorati clarissimi viri cum militiae tum civilium munerum verissumi sic auctoris quantum tamen tam arduum exsemplum sic ex intervallio sic consequi posset nihil splendidius sibi at sic dignitatem testimonio tanti praesidis nihil gratius ad memoriam amore nostro esse crediderit seu nos ab solitis iniuris pecorumque iactura laeniter sic quidem et sub imagine suae quietis vindicando seu in rationibus publicis et disceptatorem nec dissolute benignum et iustum sene sic acerbitate praebendo et quaestiones pari hominum ac rei publicae iniuria tenebris vetustatis obscuras sagaciter inlustrando pure diiudicando seu municipium infestioribus locis maximo murorum opere minimo sumptu ambiendo seu annonae avaris difficultatibus ex copis armaturae suae plurima ad nostram utilitatem nihil at sic militum damnum commodando iam quae bona animi essent verissumi sic ultra praecedentium finem moderatum verecundum mitem pudicum ordinis reverentem populi amantem sui diligentem ita liberam copiam silvarum et agrorum praebuisse ut pro tutela operantium frequens excubaret ita in cetera omni elegantia vitae aequabilem egisse ut promisquo sic usu rerum omnium occasione benefaciendi non potestate praecederet atque eandem comitatem praesenti dexteritate iucundam futuro post exsemplo sic salubrem experiremur pro quibus rebus ac moribus et pridem nos Sulpicio Felici decurionatum gradum IIviralem quot sic tunc optumus praeses libenter audierit decrevisse et nunc nominato successore cum probinquom sic eiius sic discessum ita trepide cogitemus ut instantis desideri conscientiam plenissima spe processuum eius vix lenire possimus placere erga talem virum non volgata sic decernere cum is non volgata sic praestiterit deprecarique praesidem indulgentissumum sic et praefectorum suorum quos ipse laudabiles faciat laude et laudatione laetissumum sic ut in quo grati esse voluimus faveat nobis permittendo statuam ponere praefecto suo condecurioni nostro civi egregio ne amittamus hac eum cuiius sic beneficia tenemus ac ne ubi hunc ipsum honestissumi sic quique ob curatam pulchre rem publicam statua honoraverint ibi praemium ipsius rei publicae parum gratae requiratur permittat praeterea per legatos aput sic sacratissimum principem celebrare fuisse aput sic nos Sulpicium Felicem eum praefectum qui augustissumo sic saeculo sub sanctissuma sic Uttedi Honorati discipulina sic esse debuerit quem publicum atfectum sic atsistente sic universo ordine et populo perferrent at sic amplissimum praesidem Fabius Fidus et Valerius Polio planumque imprimis facerent nos non omnia quae deberemus honoribus Sulpici Felicis decrevisse set sic quae secundum verecundiam ipsius decernere sola possimus honoribus Sulpici Felicis decrevisse set sic quae secundum verecundiam ipsius decernere sola possimus\n
EDCS-57900121.translation: The members of the council of Salernum and the people, for their affection towards the municipality of Salernum and his innocence, presented this gift to Marcus Sulpicius Felix, son of Marcus, from Rome, of the Quirina tribe, liberator and patron, who served as prefect of the I Cohort of Germans, military tribune of the XVI Flavia Firma Legion, military tribune of the III Cohort Ulpia M(i)lliaria Petraeorum, elected and retained to collect census data in parts of Armenia and Cappadocia, and prefect of the II Ala of Syrian citizens of Rome. The decree of the council is also included: Marcus Valerius Fabullus, Lucius Fabius Vivatianus, Marcus Antonius Bassianus, Caius Valerius Saturninus, Caius Fabius Fidus, Lucius Fabius Fabianus, Caius Cassius Saturninus, Lucius Valerius Gallus, Caius Valerius Rogatus, Publius Postumius Hermesander, Caius Antonius Priscus, Caius Fabius Modestus, Caius Valerius Avitus, Marcus Valerius Capito, Quintus Pontius Capitolinus, Lucius Hortensius Maurus, Quintus Fabius Pudens, Marcus Junius Cassianus, Quintus Herennius Tuscus, Quintus Fabius Fabricianus, Quintus Valerius Martialis, Marcus Fabius Virillio, Caius Annius Capito, Caius Junius Cassianus, Lucius Valerius Cornelius Saturninus, Marcus Valerius Polio, Quintus Junius Rufinianus, Sextus Antonius Bassinus, Marcus Cassius Cassianus, quaestor Titus Cassius Bassianus, Titus Aelius Africanus, Caius Atilius Zubbaeus, Lucius Cornelius Victor, Cnaeus Cornelius Cornelianus, Cnaeus Valerius Primus, Quintus Fabius Amandus, Quintus Valerius Saturninus, and Titus Aelius Zosimus, with Lollins Avitus and Statilius Maximus as consuls, on the fifth day before the Kalends of November, in the Ulpian curia, with the council of Salernum present. Caius Valerius Rogatus and Publius Postumius Hermesander, the IIviri, reported that for the most excellent and rare prefect, Sulpicius Felix, a successor had been appointed, and as his departure approaches, we are so anxious and fearful, that we can scarcely alleviate the awareness of our pressing need with the full hope of his future endeavors. It seems fitting that we should not only honor such a man with common praise, since he has rendered uncommon services, but also implore the most indulgent governor, along with his prefects whom he himself makes praiseworthy, to favor us by permitting us to erect a statue to our fellow decurion, an outstanding citizen, lest we lose him whose benefits we hold. And lest, where those who honored the most distinguished public affairs with a statue should seek a reward for the republic itself, which is not sufficiently grateful, he permit, through envoys, to celebrate before the most sacred prince that Sulpicius Felix was such a prefect as should have been under the most distinguished teacher Uttedius Honoratus in this most august age, whom the public affection, with the entire council and people standing by, and the most ample governor Fabius Fidus and Valerius Polius would make clear, that we have not decreed all the honors that we owe to Sulpicius Felix, but only those which, according to his modesty, we can decree.
EDCS-57900121.summary: This is a lengthy decree from the municipality of Salernum honoring Marcus Sulpicius Felix, their former prefect and patron, detailing his many public services and requesting permission to erect a statue in his honor, likely dating to the period of Hadrian or the Antonines based on the mention of consular names and titles.

EDCS-74700029.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely an imperial dedication or ownership mark, identifying Hadrian Augustus.

EDCS-74700030.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely an imperial dedication or ownership mark, identifying Antoninus Augustus.

EDCS-78800176.translation: For the safety and victory of the kingdom, they dedicated [this] to the god Manafes and the god Theandrios, son of Manafes.
EDCS-78800176.summary: This is a Greek votive inscription dedicating something for the safety and victory of the kingdom to two deities, Manafes and Theandrios, likely from the eastern Roman Empire where cults like this were present.
